critical_failures: Treats the raw request as an approved analytical specification.
Leaves an active question without population, grain, denominator, method, validation, or required data.
Promotes a claim beyond the stated evidence ceiling.
Omits a quality route that could materially reverse the conclusion.
Asks the requester to design inferable metrics or methods instead of proposing them.
Recommends action from observational evidence without a hypothesis, test, or limitation.

cases.raw_request: Marketing wants a list of customers likely to leave next quarter. Use everything we know, especially whether they cancelled, and show the top twenty reasons so the team can call them tomorrow. A score and a colourful dashboard would be ideal.
required_reasoning: Infer the outreach decision and intervention capacity.
Reject post-outcome cancellation fields as leakage.
Define prediction time, horizon, eligible active population, and outcome label.
necessary_context: Cost of false positives and false negatives
excluded_analysis: Decorative reason ranking before model validity
Causal retention claims from feature importance
cases.claim_ceiling: Predictive risk for an eligible future population; no causal explanation.
cases.material_clarification: Confirm only intervention constraints or consequences that materially change the target and threshold.

cases.raw_request: Operations needs next month's volume by day to set staffing. Last year's spreadsheet should be enough, but there is also a newer system with partial history. Please choose the most accurate model and give one exact number for every day.
required_reasoning: Infer the staffing decision and useful forecast horizon.
Reconcile source authority and structural change.
Replace false precision with intervals and decision-relevant error measures.
excluded_analysis: Model selection by in-sample fit
A single point forecast without uncertainty
cases.claim_ceiling: Forecast with quantified uncertainty under observed conditions.
cases.material_clarification: Confirm staffing loss asymmetry only if it changes the evaluation metric.

cases.raw_request: Can you categorise new support messages into our current team names? The historical queue field is available, although agents often move tickets around. Accuracy should be above ninety-five percent and the output needs to go straight into routing.
required_reasoning: Infer whether the construct is initial owner, final resolver, or topic.
Treat historical queues as noisy labels.
Define abstention and human-review paths.
excluded_analysis: Accepting ninety-five percent accuracy without a baseline
Using final queue as an unquestioned label
cases.claim_ceiling: Classification performance on a representative holdout under the reviewed label definition.
cases.material_clarification: Confirm the operational action when two teams are both plausible.

cases.raw_request: Risk wants every incoming case labelled low, medium, or high so the high ones can be handled first. There are past analyst labels, but no written policy, and management would prefer the model to agree with senior reviewers.
required_reasoning: Infer the prioritisation decision rather than copying reviewer habits.
Require a usable label policy and disagreement review.
Define asymmetric error costs and escalation.
excluded_analysis: Treating seniority as ground truth
Automated deployment before label governance
cases.claim_ceiling: Reviewed decision-support classification; no autonomous high-impact decision.
cases.material_clarification: Obtain accountable approval for the class policy and human override.

cases.raw_request: Finance says daily completed orders look strange. Flag every day more than ten percent from the average, explain what caused it, and compare the warehouse report with the commerce dashboard because their totals differ.
required_reasoning: Infer whether the decision is investigation, correction, or monitoring.
Replace a global average with a seasonal baseline.
Separate reconciliation from cause analysis.
excluded_analysis: Calling every ten-percent deviation anomalous
Claiming a cause from temporal coincidence
cases.claim_ceiling: Statistically unusual relative to a validated baseline; causes remain hypotheses unless independently supported.
cases.material_clarification: Confirm the alert action and acceptable false-positive burden if not inferable.

cases.raw_request: Engineering wants one chart that spots unusual latency across all services and regions. Alert whenever anything is outside the normal range, but avoid noisy alerts and make sure a newly launched service is covered immediately.
required_reasoning: Infer the incident-response decision and alert hierarchy.
Handle multiple comparisons and sparse new-service history.
Define baseline fallback and alert review.
excluded_analysis: One undifferentiated threshold
Claiming coverage for a service with no baseline
cases.claim_ceiling: Operational anomaly signal with documented sensitivity and false-positive limits.
cases.material_clarification: Confirm escalation policy for critical services.

cases.raw_request: Product has four hundred comments copied from a survey and wants the five biggest complaints plus quotes for tomorrow's roadmap meeting. The comments are mostly from people who chose to answer after a redesign.
required_reasoning: Infer whether the decision needs theme discovery or prevalence estimates.
Separate corpus frequency from customer prevalence.
Require negative cases and review of the codebook.
excluded_analysis: Calling corpus shares customer shares
Selecting only vivid supporting quotes
cases.claim_ceiling: Themes present in the covered response corpus; prevalence outside it is unknown.
cases.material_clarification: No clarification unless roadmap choices require a population prevalence estimate.

cases.raw_request: Please combine meeting notes, chat transcripts, and closed support tickets to find what customers care about. Rank the themes by importance and show whether sentiment improved, even though each source covers a different period and audience.
required_reasoning: Infer the decision and define importance before ranking.
Keep source populations visible rather than pooling incompatible corpora.
Define stable coding and time-comparable sentiment.
excluded_analysis: Naive pooled frequency
A sentiment trend across non-comparable corpora
cases.claim_ceiling: Source-bounded qualitative themes and cautious within-source comparisons.
cases.material_clarification: Confirm the decision meaning of importance if several definitions would change priorities.

cases.raw_request: Growth wants proof that people who watch the tutorial buy more. Compare watchers with non-watchers, break it down by channel, and recommend making the tutorial mandatory if the uplift is positive.
required_reasoning: Reframe proof as an observational association unless a causal design exists.
Define exposure before outcome and eligible opportunity.
Test channel and intent composition.
excluded_analysis: Full-period exposure that occurs after purchase
Mandatory rollout recommendation from raw uplift
cases.claim_ceiling: Association after measured adjustment; causal effect requires an experiment or credible causal design.
cases.material_clarification: Confirm whether an experiment is operationally possible.

cases.raw_request: Compare the journeys of people who completed the outcome with everyone else and tell us which content makes them convert. We have event data, session recordings from a sample, and a long list of clicks the stakeholder considers important.
required_reasoning: Separate descriptive content consumption from outcome comparison.
Stop behavioural exposure at the first outcome.
Validate requested clicks against user-visible constructs.
excluded_analysis: Post-outcome behaviour in explanatory comparisons
Treating sampled recordings as prevalence estimates
cases.claim_ceiling: Descriptive and associative journey differences; content effects remain test hypotheses.
cases.material_clarification: Confirm only materially ambiguous outcome rules or business actions.

cases.raw_request: The dashboard says eighty percent completion is low. Make a funnel by completion band, compare mobile and desktop, and explain where users drop. People with no tracked completion event are not currently in the export.
required_reasoning: Distinguish exclusive maximum-depth classes from cumulative reach.
Restore eligible zero-state cases to the denominator.
Reject a funnel for mutually exclusive classes.
excluded_analysis: A funnel using exclusive bands
A denominator containing only tracked completers
cases.claim_ceiling: Descriptive distribution and reach; no reason for drop-off without diagnostic evidence.
cases.material_clarification: No clarification if eligibility can be established from an authoritative source.

cases.raw_request: The team keeps adding optional content and wants to know what is consumed. There are aggregate interaction events, a few browser inspections, and a second tool that samples sessions. Please map every content area to a percentage and rank what should be removed.
required_reasoning: Infer the content decision and separate availability, visibility, interaction, and consumption.
Use browser evidence to define constructs, not prevalence.
Avoid removal recommendations without outcome or experiment evidence.
excluded_analysis: One denominator for unavailable optional content
Pixel observations generalised to all experiences
Automatic removal ranking
cases.claim_ceiling: Descriptive consumption within measured availability and coverage; optimisation ideas are hypotheses.
cases.material_clarification: Confirm the intended content decision only if keep, reorder, and remove imply different success criteria.
